title: SA rotation lifecycle — timeline from creation through expiry
description: Reference timeline illustrating the mandatory-rotation lifecycle. Every transition is guarded by the observer or a scheduled job. Times are relative to creation (T=0) and use the module-default TTL (90d) + warning window (14d) + grace period (7d).

timeline.notes: The `pending` status persists until the SA makes its first token-exchange. Plaintext secret returned ONCE in the response body.

timeline.actor: the SA itself (bcrypt secret → HS256 JWT via /api/v1/service-accounts/token)
timeline.notes: status transitions from `pending` to `active` on first successful exchange. All subsequent exchanges + verifications update last_used_at without changing status.

Job enumerates every SA whose expires_at falls inside the 14-day warning window and fires this event per SA. The event is debounced weekly so a single SA sees at most 3 reminders across the 14-day window.
Second reminder. Operators who ignored the first should now escalate.

Rotation refreshes secret_hash + secret_rotated_at + expires_at atomically. Plaintext returned ONCE. The signer_kid is UNCHANGED — kid rotation is a separate operation coordinated with the auth module.

expires_at reached. JWTs signed BEFORE this moment remain valid until natural exp (default 300s = 5min). Token-exchange refuses new secret-swap with 401 INVALID_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_CREDENTIALS (the audit-log line captures SERVICE_ACCOUNT_SECRET_EXPIRED).

Two events fire in the same transaction: (a) ServiceAccountExpired (informational, gauge + alert), (b) ServiceAccountDisabled with trigger='expired'. Dispatch ServiceAccountExpiredNotification to Application owners + security-mailing-list.

timeline.event: — (SA still present, is_enabled=false, expires_at long past)
timeline.notes: An expired SA remains in the table indefinitely until manually deleted. Ops that ignored the disable can either rotate (re-enable) OR delete.
Soft-delete starts the retention hold. is_enabled is force-set to false in the same transaction as deleted_at.

Job's pass 2 hard-deletes the row. Audit rows referencing this SA get their user_id anonymised to `[REDACTED-SERVICE-ACCOUNT]`. The signer_kid is NOT touched — the auth module owns kid retention independently.

invariants: Every state transition fires an after-commit event so downstream listeners never see uncommitted state.
The plaintext secret is generated + returned ONCE, at create + rotate. Never retrievable again — lost secret = mandatory rotation.

The signer_kid does NOT rotate on secret rotation. Kid rotation is a separate operation orchestrated by auth::JwtSigningKeyRotator and cascades to this module via the HandleSignerKidRotation listener.
Existing JWTs signed BEFORE any lifecycle transition (rotation, disable, expiry, delete) remain valid until their natural exp — hard revocation requires kid burn via auth::JwtSigningKeyDisabled.
PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob's two-pass daily sweep is idempotent — running it twice in a day (backfill) yields the same state as running it once.
